Seikyo Holdings KK — Internal Legal & Compliance Memo (Board Circulation)
To: Board of Directors; From: Ms. Rika Suda, General Counsel; Date: 31 May 2026; Subject: APPI/My Number compliance, FIEA MNPI controls, and Hanabi Systems KK acquisition.
Summary: The proposed acquisition of Hanabi Systems KK (private issuer) and concurrent employee share plan onboarding implicate APPI and FIEA workflows.
APPI/My Number: Employee onboarding form FY26 omitted My Number fields; collection is not required for securities grant processing, and the prior pilot form was decommissioned with all test entries erased on 15 May 2026.
DSAR: One data subject access request from Mr. Kenji Aoba (contract engineer) was fulfilled and closed; payroll contact identifiers were provided, and the minimum necessary copy was retained per Retention Schedule RS-7.
Consent withdrawal: Ms. Miki Tamura withdrew marketing-email consent on 20 May; withdrawal did not affect employment processing, and the HR notice was logged for audit only.
Cross-border transfer: Data-Export Approval Form DE-14 approved transfer of limited employee contact data to Mirai Processors Pte. Ltd. (SG) under DPA Schedule 2; identifiers were minimised and no My Number was exported.
Erasure: Former employee My Number hashes were already purged by the Secure Archive Job SA-19; this is a completed control and does not indicate a live risk.
Retention: Board confirms adherence to RS-7 (18 months for onboarding metadata, 7 years for statutory payroll records, no retention for unnecessary identifiers).
FIEA MNPI: Undisclosed enumerated material facts exist regarding the Hanabi Systems KK tender offer analysis; no tender-offer announcement is contemplated at this time, and insiders remain under blackout per Policy FI-3.
Securities workflow: Draft internal issuance codes for the employee share plan are for internal tracking only and will not be disseminated externally; IR briefings will be routed through the Nihon Pacific Exchange (NPX) listing agent if listing proceeds.
Governance: Compliance Liaison Desk (privacy@seikyo-holdings.co.jp) remains the single point of contact; the Whistle Contact Portal is for completed reporting only and is not a DSAR intake.
Vendor DPA: Mirai Processors Pte. Ltd. signed DPA Schedule 1–3; encryption-at-rest and role-based access are active, and the cross-border transfer relies on contractual clauses consistent with APPI.
Negative control: The “Global Marketing Cloud” rollout is paused and not processing any personal data; references in old project minutes shall not be treated as current risk indicators.
Action items: Board to note MNPI handling, reconfirm blackout observance, and approve the retention adjustments; no further APPI/My Number collection changes are requested at this stage.
